title: React Basics Knowledge Check
description: Check your understanding of React components, JSX, props, and state before moving on.
author: DailyCoder

questions.prompt: What does JSX ultimately compile down to?
questions.explanation: JSX is syntactic sugar; a compiler (e.g. Babel) transforms it into React.createElement calls that return React elements.

questions.prompt: How do you pass data from a parent component down to a child component?

options.text: You can't pass data to children

questions.explanation: Props are the mechanism for passing read-only data from a parent to a child component.

questions.prompt: Updating a component's state triggers a re-render of that component.
questions.explanation: When state changes via the setter, React schedules a re-render so the UI reflects the new state.

questions.prompt: Which hook lets a function component hold local state?
questions.explanation: useState returns the current state value and a setter function for updating it.

questions.prompt: Which statements about React components are correct? (Select all that apply.)

options.text: Component names must start with a capital letter
Props are read-only inside the receiving component
A component can return multiple root elements without a wrapper or Fragment
Components can be composed by nesting them in JSX

questions.explanation: Component names are capitalized so JSX treats them as components, props are immutable in the child, and components compose by nesting. A component must return a single root (an element or Fragment).
